headerDisplay: See the README **Setup** section for how to obtain these values. **IMPORTANT:** when you authorize the account you MUST use the special authorization URL that includes the Pub/Sub scope (`+https://www.googleapis.com/auth/pubsub`), otherwise device events (motion, doorbell, temperature changes) will not work.

clientId.title: OAuth Client ID
clientId.description: From Google Cloud Platform. Ends in '.apps.googleusercontent.com' (e.g. "780816631155-gbvyo1o7r2pn95qc4ei9d61io4uh48hl.apps.googleusercontent.com"). NOT the Device Access project UUID.

clientSecret.title: OAuth Client Secret
clientSecret.description: From Google Cloud Platform, paired with the Client ID above (starts with 'GOCSPX-').

projectId.title: Device Access Project ID
projectId.description: The UUID from the Device Access Console (e.g. "4f689e03-..."). This is the SDM 'project' and is NOT your Google Cloud project.

refreshToken.title: Refresh Token
refreshToken.description: Obtained during account authorization. Must be generated using the authorization URL that includes the Pub/Sub scope (see the header note), or events will not work.

gcpProjectId.title: Google Cloud Project ID
gcpProjectId.description: Your Google Cloud project ID (e.g. "my-project-334315") — the one the Pub/Sub subscription lives under. Distinct from the Device Access Project ID above.

vEncoder.title: Video Encoder
vEncoder.description: ffmpeg video encoder for camera streams. Default is software 'libx264'. For hardware transcoding set e.g. 'h264_qsv' (Intel Quick Sync), 'h264_videotoolbox' (macOS), 'h264_v4l2m2m'/'h264_vaapi' (Linux), or 'h264_nvenc' (NVIDIA) — your ffmpeg build must include the chosen encoder (see ffmpegPath).

ffmpegPath.title: Custom ffmpeg Path
ffmpegPath.description: Absolute path to an ffmpeg binary. Leave blank to use the bundled ffmpeg-for-homebridge, then a system 'ffmpeg'. Set this to point at a build that includes your chosen hardware encoder.

snapshotRefresh.title: Refresh Camera Snapshots
snapshotRefresh.description: Periodically grab a still from cameras (on motion, and when you open the Home app) so tiles show a recent image instead of a placeholder. Grabs are serialized and rate-limited to avoid Google SDM 429 throttling.

snapshotRefreshOnAppOpen.title: Refresh On App Open
snapshotRefreshOnAppOpen.description: Also top up stale tiles when the Home app requests snapshots. More current thumbnails, but each grab is a stream start that counts toward Google SDM rate limits. Disable to refresh on motion only.

snapshotRefreshSpacing.title: Snapshot Refresh Spacing (seconds)
snapshotRefreshSpacing.description: Minimum seconds between snapshot grabs across all cameras. Lower = fresher tiles but closer to the SDM rate limit (HTTP 429).

snapshotRefreshTtl.title: Snapshot Freshness (minutes)
snapshotRefreshTtl.description: A tile newer than this is considered fresh and won't be re-grabbed.

fanDuration.title: Fan duration (in seconds) when turning on the fan.
